commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Mad. Israel shall promise itself all prosperity, not being inspired by God, but full of madness. (Worthington)
There were many false prophets. The true ones were often accounted fools, 4 Kings ix. 11., and Ezechiel iii. 25. (Calmet)
"What is said respecting Israel, in this prophet, must be understood of heretics, who being truly mad, utter falsehoods against God. "(St. Jerome)
Septuagint, "and Israel shall be hurt like the prophet beside himself, the man having (or hurried away by) the spirit. "(Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
